compiled_state.title: Reconciler: derive status via the reducer, not applier._read_latest_status
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
Rec 7a of session-logs/2026-06-09-architecture-review.md (risk R6). applier.py:640 _read_latest_status re-implements status replay as a raw scan of STATUS event files ("last STATUS json wins"). It ignores SNAPSHOT folding and the reducer's UUID-based fork resolution, so it can disagree with the reducer on a forked or compacted ticket — the reconciler may then push a wrong status to Jira. Latent correctness bug, not a refactor.

Work: replace _read_latest_status with reduce_ticket(...)["status"] (or a thin reducer-backed helper). RED test first: construct a store with (a) a compacted ticket (SNAPSHOT + retired events) and (b) a STATUS fork resolved by UUID, show _read_latest_status diverging from the reducer, then fix.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] RED test demonstrating divergence on compacted and forked tickets, then GREEN via reducer-derived status
- [ ] _read_latest_status deleted or reduced to a reducer call
- [ ] Reconciler unit suites green

comments.body:
IMPLEMENTATION NOTES (experimentally validated in sandbox, 2026-06-09) — EMPIRICAL FACTS AN EXECUTOR NEEDS:
1. Call site + path resolution: _read_latest_status(tracker_dir, ticket_id) is called from the inbound STATUS leaf at applier.py:999; tracker dir from _resolve_tracker_dir(repo_root) (applier.py:630; TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR env override else <repo_root>/.tickets-tracker).
2. Reducer signature: ticket_reducer.reduce_ticket(ticket_dir_path, strategy=None) takes the PER-TICKET dir (<tracker>/<ticket_id>), not the tracker root.
3. Validated replacement body: keep the not-a-dir -> "open" early return; then
    try:
        from ticket_reducer import reduce_ticket  # engine dir on sys.path in production
        state = reduce_ticket(str(ticket_dir))
    except Exception:
        return "open"
    return (state.get("status") or "open") if isinstance(state, dict) else "open"
   (In-function import with fallback because reconciler modules are sometimes spec-loaded under test; production always has the engine dir on sys.path via engine_env / python3 -m rebar_reconciler.)
4. Compacted layout (learned empirically — write the RED fixture this way): after rebar compact, the ticket dir contains ONLY the <ts>-<uuid>-SNAPSHOT.json event (originals are REMOVED, not .retired siblings); data.compiled_state.status holds the real status; data.source_event_uuids lists absorbed events. Surprise: closing a ticket can auto-compact via the lifecycle path — a freshly closed ticket may already be SNAPSHOT-only.
5. Fork fixture trap: a naive fork WITHOUT parent_status_uuid makes old and new impls coincidentally agree (reducer's empty-existing-uuid rule, bug e60b). The RED fork fixture needs realistic events: prior STATUS pppp0001, then two same-timestamp siblings aaaa1111->closed / zzzz9999->blocked, both parent_status_uuid=pppp0001. OLD scan -> blocked (last filename); NEW -> closed (= reducer).
6. Validated divergences: compacted closed ticket OLD->open vs NEW->closed; realistic fork as above. Missing ticket -> "open" both (contract preserved). Regression: full tests/unit/rebar_reconciler -> 862 passed, 2 xfailed against the patch.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
